talk_objective_responses.text: Ah, thank you for bringing this to me. You've done well.
I see. Thank you for the report. This information is valuable.
Very good. I appreciate you taking the time to speak with me about this.

merchant: The job's done then? Excellent work. Here's your payment - earned fair and square.
Ah, you've returned! And successful too. Business is always better when contracts get fulfilled.
Well handled, friend. Take your coin and know you've earned a good reputation here.
guard: Report acknowledged. The watch appreciates your service to the realm.
Good. This matter is now resolved. The guard captain will be pleased.
Duty fulfilled. You've done the right thing. Here's your reward.
scholar: Fascinating! You've succeeded where others failed. I shall make note of this for my research.
The task is complete? Excellent. Knowledge gained through action is knowledge well-earned.
Most illuminating. You've proven quite capable. Accept this with my gratitude.
priest: The gods smile upon your deeds this day. Accept their blessing along with this reward.
You've done holy work, friend. May this payment serve you well in your continued devotion.
Blessings upon you. The faithful are grateful for your service to the community.
innkeeper: Ha! Knew you'd come through. Drinks are on the house tonight, and here's your payment.
Back in one piece! That's always a relief. Here's what I owe you, with thanks.
Well done! The regulars will be glad to hear it. Take your coin and rest your feet.
farmer: You've done right by us. It's not much, but here's what we promised.
The fields are safer now thanks to you. Take this with our humble gratitude.
Honest work deserves honest pay. You've earned every coin of this.
default: You've done well. The task is complete.
Good work. Here's what was promised.
The job's finished then. Take your reward with my thanks.

responses.text: Work? Why would I help the likes of you find work?
I'm not in the business of helping strangers. Ask someone else.
Check the bounty board near the town square. They post notices there for all sorts of work - monster hunts, deliveries, the usual.
The bounty board's where you want to look. Last I saw, they were offering good coin for clearing out some goblins.
The guild hall's your best bet for steady work. Fighters' guild especially - they're always looking for capable folk.
Head to the guild hall. The fighters take on muscle, the mages need research done, and the thieves... well, they don't advertise openly, if you take my meaning.
The temple sometimes needs help with... delicate matters. The priests won't say it openly, but they pay well for those who can handle themselves.
The merchants are always looking for guards or couriers. Talk to the traders near the market - they'll pay for safe passage of their goods.
A merchant was complaining about missing shipments just yesterday. Might be worth asking around if you're looking for paying work.
There's work aplenty for adventurers, but be careful what you take on. Plenty of folk have bitten off more than they could chew and never came back.
A word of advice - avoid the jobs marked with a red seal on the bounty board. Those are the ones that got the last adventurer killed.
If someone offers you work that sounds too good to be true, it probably is. I've seen eager adventurers walk into traps more times than I can count.
Adventurers come through here all the time looking for work. Most head to the guild hall or the tavern. That's where the job offers flow.
Saw a group of adventurers head out toward the old ruins yesterday. Wonder if they'll be the ones to finally clear whatever's lurking there.
You've got the look of someone seeking adventure. This town's got plenty of troubles that could use a capable hand, if you're willing.
The captain of the guard sometimes posts special jobs. He's usually at the barracks near the north gate. Pays better than the bounty board, but harder work too.
Old Henrick at the tavern knows everyone's business. If there's work to be had, he'll know who's offering and what it pays.
The roads haven't been safe lately. Caravans are paying double for guards. Dangerous work, but the coin's good.
Word is the local mine needs clearing. Something moved in and the miners won't go back until it's dealt with. Good opportunity for someone with steel in their spine.
The herbalist needs rare ingredients from the old forest. Dangerous journey, but she pays in potions and remedies - might be worth more than coin in the long run.
